method_atom: Equilibrium and out-of-equilibrium statistical mechanics of many interacting units whose pairwise coupling is non-integrable (decays too slowly to be additive), making the system non-additive and its accessible macroscopic-parameter space possibly non-convex; this yields inequivalence between the fixed-energy and fixed-control-parameter statistical ensembles (negative response coefficients, discontinuous jumps at first-order transitions), generic breaking of phase-space accessibility, and anomalously slow relaxation through long-lived quasi-stationary states, analyzed exactly in mean-field models and via a self-consistent kinetic (collisionless mean-field transport) equation for the one-particle distribution.
object_atom: Physical many-body systems whose two-body potential decays as V(r) ~ 1/r^alpha with alpha <= d in d spatial dimensions, such as self-gravitating systems, two-dimensional hydrodynamics and elasticity, and charged or dipolar systems.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the ensemble-inequivalence/non-additivity statistical mechanics plus mean-field kinetic (Vlasov-type) relaxation theory; what is domain-specific is the class of physical long-range-potential systems (gravity, plasmas, 2D fluids) to which it is applied.
